sections.paragraph:
πολυπράγμων, ον, gen. ονος (cp. prec. entry; since Eupolis Com. [V b.c.] 222, Lysias, Aristoph.; Philo Bybl. [100 a.d.]: 790 Fgm. 1 [24] Jac. [in Eu., PE 1, 9, 24]; Philo Alex., Abr. 20, Spec. Leg. 1, 69; Jos., Ant. 15, 182) if in the rarer good sense (Polyb. 9, 4, 4; cp. πολυπραγμονέω ‘inquire’ New Docs 1 no. 12, 15) inquisitive π. ἄνθρωποι Dg 5:3.—DELG s.v. πράσσω.

gen. gen. = genitive

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

prec. prec. = preceding

Eupolis Com Eupolis Com , V b.c.—List 5

Lysias Lysias , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Philo Bybl. Philo Bybl. = P. of Byblos, hist., c. 100 a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 847
